checks.shortDescription: A method that overrides a method in a superclass but does not have an 'Override' annotation cannot take advantage of compiler checks, and makes code less readable.
checks.title: Missing Override annotation

checks.shortDescription: A field of immutable type that is assigned to only in a constructor or static initializer of its declaring type, but is not declared 'final', may lead to defects and makes code...
checks.title: Non-final immutable field

checks.shortDescription: A non-constant field that is not declared 'private', but is not accessed outside of its declaring type, may decrease code maintainability.
checks.title: Non-private field

checks.shortDescription: Using a method or constructor that has been marked as deprecated may be dangerous or fail to take advantage of a better method or constructor.
checks.title: Deprecated method or constructor invocation

checks.shortDescription: Javadoc that incorrectly claims a method or constructor can throw an exception is misleading.
checks.title: Javadoc has impossible 'throws' tag

checks.shortDescription: A public method or constructor that does not have a Javadoc comment affects maintainability.
checks.title: Missing Javadoc for public method or constructor

checks.shortDescription: A public method or constructor that does not have a Javadoc tag for each parameter affects maintainability.
checks.title: Missing Javadoc for parameter

checks.shortDescription: A public method that does not have a Javadoc tag for its return value affects maintainability.
checks.title: Missing Javadoc for method return value

checks.shortDescription: A public method or constructor that throws an exception but does not have a Javadoc tag for the exception affects maintainability.
checks.title: Missing Javadoc for thrown exception

checks.shortDescription: A public class or interface that does not have a Javadoc comment affects maintainability.
checks.title: Missing Javadoc for public type

checks.shortDescription: Javadoc @param tags that do not match any parameters in the method or constructor or any type parameters of the annotated class are confusing.
checks.title: Spurious Javadoc @param tags

checks.shortDescription: Calling a method that overrides 'Object.clone' is bad practice.
checks.title: Use of clone() method

checks.shortDescription: Overriding 'Object.clone' is bad practice.
checks.title: Overriding definition of clone()

checks.shortDescription: Using the 'Cloneable' interface is bad practice.
checks.title: Use of Cloneable interface

checks.shortDescription: Overriding 'Object.finalize' is not a reliable way to terminate use of resources.
checks.title: Overriding definition of finalize()

checks.shortDescription: A static, final field name that contains lowercase letters decreases readability.
checks.title: Misnamed static final field

checks.shortDescription: A method name that begins with an uppercase letter decreases readability.
checks.title: Misnamed method

checks.shortDescription: A package name that contains uppercase letters decreases readability.
checks.title: Misnamed package

checks.shortDescription: A class or interface name that begins with a lowercase letter decreases readability.
checks.title: Misnamed class or interface

checks.shortDescription: A variable name that begins with an uppercase letter decreases readability.
checks.title: Misnamed variable

checks.shortDescription: A 'switch' statement that is based on a non-enumerated type and that does not have a 'default' case may allow execution to 'fall through' silently.
checks.title: Missing default case in switch

checks.shortDescription: More than one statement per line decreases readability.
checks.title: Multiple statements on line

checks.shortDescription: An 'if-else-if' statement without a terminating 'else' clause may allow execution to 'fall through' silently.
checks.title: Non-terminated if-else-if chain

checks.shortDescription: Parameterizing a call to a constructor of a generic type increases type safety and code readability.
checks.title: Non-parameterized constructor invocation

checks.shortDescription: Using a parameterized instance of a generic type for a method return type increases type safety and code readability.
checks.title: Non-parameterized method return type

checks.shortDescription: Declaring a field, parameter, or local variable as a parameterized type increases type safety and code readability.
checks.title: Non-parameterized variable

checks.shortDescription: Mutual dependency between types makes code difficult to understand and test.
checks.title: Mutually-dependent types

checks.shortDescription: Unnecessary Maven dependencies are a maintenance burden.
checks.title: Unused Maven dependency (binary)

checks.shortDescription: Unnecessary Maven dependencies are a maintenance burden.
checks.title: Unused Maven dependency (source)

checks.shortDescription: Deeply-nested classes are difficult to understand, since they have access to many scopes
checks.title: Deeply-nested class

checks.shortDescription: A method that uses more methods or variables from another (unrelated) class than from its own class violates the principle of putting data and behavior in the same place.
checks.title: Feature envy

checks.shortDescription: Hub classes, which are classes that use, and are used by, many other classes, are complex and difficult to change without affecting the rest of the system.
checks.title: Hub classes

checks.shortDescription: Two otherwise unrelated classes that share too much information about each other are difficult to maintain, change and understand.
checks.title: Inappropriate Intimacy

checks.shortDescription: Use of unsupported JDK-internal APIs may cause compatibility issues when upgrading to newer versions of Java, in particular Java 9.
checks.title: Access to unsupported JDK-internal API

checks.shortDescription: Use of a single underscore character as an identifier results in a compiler error with Java source level 9 or later.
checks.title: Underscore used as identifier

checks.shortDescription: A block that contains too many complex statements becomes unreadable and unmaintainable.
checks.title: Block with too many statements

checks.shortDescription: Very complex conditions are difficult to read and may include defects.
checks.title: Complex condition

checks.shortDescription: Dead classes add unnecessary complexity.
checks.title: Dead class

checks.shortDescription: Dead enum constants add unnecessary complexity.
checks.title: Dead enum constant

checks.shortDescription: Fields that are never read are likely unnecessary.
checks.title: Dead field

checks.shortDescription: Dead methods add unnecessary complexity.
checks.title: Dead method

checks.shortDescription: Parameters that are not used add unnecessary complexity to an interface.
checks.title: Useless parameter

checks.shortDescription: An EJB should not attempt to create a class loader, obtain the current class loader, set the context class loader, set a security manager, create a new security manager, stop the...
checks.title: EJB interferes with container operation

checks.shortDescription: An EJB should not attempt to access files or directories in the file system.
checks.title: EJB uses file input/output

checks.shortDescription: An EJB should not use AWT or other graphics functionality.
checks.title: EJB uses graphics

checks.shortDescription: An EJB should not attempt to load or execute native code.
checks.title: EJB uses native code

checks.shortDescription: An EJB should not attempt to use the Reflection API, as this could compromise security.
checks.title: EJB uses reflection

checks.shortDescription: An EJB should not attempt to access or modify any Java security configuration, including the Policy, Security, Provider, Signer and Identity objects.
checks.title: EJB accesses security configuration

checks.shortDescription: An EJB should not use the subclass or object substitution features of the Java serialization protocol, since their use could compromise security.
checks.title: EJB uses substitution in serialization

checks.shortDescription: An EJB should not set the socket factory used by ServerSocket or Socket, or the stream handler factory used by URL.
checks.title: EJB sets socket factory or URL stream handler factory

checks.shortDescription: An EJB should not attempt to listen to or accept connections on a socket, or use a socket for multicast.
checks.title: EJB uses server socket

checks.shortDescription: An EJB should not make use of non-final static fields, since a consistent state of such fields is not guaranteed if an EJB instance is distributed across multiple JVMs.
checks.title: EJB uses non-final static field

checks.shortDescription: An EJB should not use synchronization, since it will not work properly if an EJB is distributed across multiple JVMs.
checks.title: EJB uses synchronization

checks.shortDescription: An EJB should not use 'this' as a method argument or result.
checks.title: EJB uses 'this' as argument or result

checks.shortDescription: An EJB should not attempt to manage threads, as it could interfere with the EJB container's operation.
checks.title: EJB uses threads

checks.shortDescription: Beans that share similar properties exhibit unnecessary repetition in the bean definitions and make the system's architecture more difficult to see.
checks.title: Beans sharing similar properties

checks.shortDescription: Too many beans in a file can make the file difficult to understand and maintain.
checks.title: Too many beans in file

checks.shortDescription: Beans that are specified but never used are redundant and should be removed.
checks.title: Beans that are never used within the code

checks.shortDescription: A bean property that overrides the same property in a parent bean, and has the same contents, is useless.
checks.title: Useless property override

checks.shortDescription: Using autowiring in Spring beans may make it difficult to maintain large projects.
checks.title: Avoid autowiring

checks.shortDescription: Using a type name instead of an index number in a Spring 'constructor-arg' element improves readability.
checks.title: Use constructor-arg types instead of index

checks.shortDescription: Putting 'import' statements before bean definitions in a Spring bean configuration file makes it easier to immediately see all the file's dependencies.
checks.title: Imports should come before bean definitions

checks.shortDescription: Adding 'description' elements to a Spring XML bean definition file is good practice.
checks.title: This bean does not have a description element

checks.shortDescription: A non-abstract Spring bean that is a parent of other beans and specifies an abstract class causes an error during bean instantiation.
checks.title: Non-abstract parent beans should not use an abstract class

checks.shortDescription: Using 'id' instead of 'name' to name a Spring bean enables the XML parser to perform additional checks.
checks.title: Use id instead of name

checks.shortDescription: Using local references when referring to Spring beans in the same file allows reference errors to be detected during XML parsing.
checks.title: Use local refs when referring to beans in the same file

checks.shortDescription: When using the Spring Framework, using setter injection instead of constructor injection is more flexible, especially when several properties are optional.
checks.title: Use setter injection instead of constructor injection

checks.shortDescription: Using shortcut forms may make a Spring XML configuration file less cluttered.
checks.title: Use shortcut forms for values

checks.shortDescription: Not declaring a setter for a property that is defined in a Spring XML file causes a compilation error.
checks.title: Missing setters for property dependency injection

checks.shortDescription: Casting 'this' to a type parameter of the current type masks an implicit type constraint that should be explicitly stated.
checks.title: Cast of 'this' to a type parameter

checks.shortDescription: Long sequences of type tests on a variable are difficult to maintain.
checks.title: Chain of 'instanceof' tests

checks.shortDescription: Casting 'this' to a derived type introduces a dependency cycle between the type of 'this' and the target type.
checks.title: Dubious downcast of 'this'

checks.shortDescription: Testing whether 'this' is an instance of a derived type introduces a dependency cycle between the type of 'this' and the target type.
checks.title: Dubious type test of 'this'

checks.shortDescription: An empty method serves no purpose and makes code less readable.
checks.title: Empty method

checks.shortDescription: Using 'enum' as an identifier makes the code incompatible with Java 5 and later.
checks.title: String 'enum' used as identifier

checks.shortDescription: Extending or implementing an annotation is unlikely to be what the programmer intends.
checks.title: Annotation is extended or implemented

checks.shortDescription: An 'Iterator' that also implements 'Iterable' by returning itself as its 'Iterator' does not support multiple traversals.
checks.title: Iterator implementing Iterable

checks.shortDescription: A non-case label appearing in a switch statement is confusing to read or may even indicate a bug.
checks.title: Non-case label in switch statement

checks.shortDescription: An 'if' statement where both branches either (a) return or (b) write to the same variable can often be expressed more clearly using the '?' operator.
checks.title: Missed ternary opportunity

checks.shortDescription: A method that appears to override another method but does not, because the declaring classes are in different packages, is potentially confusing.
checks.title: Confusing non-overriding of package-private method

checks.shortDescription: If 'C' is a final class, a type bound such as '?
checks.title: Type bound extends a final class

checks.shortDescription: A type variable with the same name as another type that is in scope can cause the two types to be confused.
checks.title: Type variable hides another type

checks.shortDescription: Checking whether an expression is null when that expression cannot possibly be null is useless.
checks.title: Useless null check

checks.shortDescription: Testing whether a derived type is an instance of its base type is unnecessary.
checks.title: Useless type test

checks.shortDescription: Upcasting a derived type to its base type is usually unnecessary.
checks.title: Useless upcast

checks.shortDescription: An 'Iterable' that reuses an 'Iterator' instance does not support multiple traversals and can lead to unexpected behavior.
checks.title: Iterable wrapping an iterator

checks.shortDescription: Calling 'Math.abs' to find the absolute value of a randomly generated integer is not guaranteed to return a non-negative integer.
checks.title: Incorrect absolute value of random number

checks.shortDescription: Code that uses 'x % 2 == 1' or 'x % 2 > 0' to check whether a number is odd does not work for negative numbers.
checks.title: Bad parity check

checks.shortDescription: Using the '(p?e1:e2)' operator with different primitive types for the second and third operands may cause unexpected results.
checks.title: Type mismatch in conditional expression

checks.shortDescription: An expression that always evaluates to the same value, but which has a non-constant subexpression, indicates a mistake.
checks.title: Expression always evaluates to the same value

checks.shortDescription: Compound assignment statements (for example 'intvar += longvar') that implicitly cast a value of a wider type to a narrower type may result in information loss and numeric errors...
checks.title: Implicit narrowing conversion in compound assignment

checks.shortDescription: Casting the result of a multiplication to a wider type instead of casting before the multiplication may cause overflow.
checks.title: Result of multiplication cast to wider type

checks.shortDescription: Left-shifting an integer by more than its type width indicates a mistake.
checks.title: Left shift by more than the type width

checks.shortDescription: Using the remainder operator with the multiplication operator without adding parentheses to clarify precedence may cause confusion.
checks.title: Multiplication of remainder

checks.shortDescription: An integer literal that starts with '0' may cause a problem.
checks.title: Use of octal values

checks.shortDescription: Creating an instance of 'Random' for each pseudo-random number required does not guarantee an evenly distributed sequence of random numbers.
checks.title: Random used only once

checks.shortDescription: Nested expressions where the formatting contradicts the grouping enforced by operator precedence are difficult to read and may even indicate a bug.
checks.title: Whitespace contradicts operator precedence

checks.shortDescription: A 'clone' method that is overridden in a subclass, and that does not itself call 'super.clone', causes calls to the subclass's 'clone' method to return an object of the wrong type.
checks.title: Missing super clone

checks.shortDescription: A class that implements 'Cloneable' but does not override the 'clone' method will have undesired behavior.
checks.title: No clone method

checks.shortDescription: Accessing an array with an index that is greater than or equal to the length of the array causes an 'ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException'.
checks.title: Array index out of bounds

checks.shortDescription: Calling container access methods such as 'Collection.contains' or 'Map.get' with an object of a type that is incompatible with the corresponding container element type is...
checks.title: Type mismatch on container access

checks.shortDescription: Attempting to invoke 'Iterator.remove' on an iterator over a collection that does not support element removal causes a runtime exception.
checks.title: Call to Iterator.remove may fail

checks.shortDescription: Querying the contents of a collection or map that is never initialized is not normally useful.
checks.title: Container contents are never initialized

checks.shortDescription: Calling container modification methods such as 'Collection.remove' or 'Map.remove' with an object of a type that is incompatible with the corresponding container element type is...
checks.title: Type mismatch on container modification

checks.shortDescription: A collection or map whose contents are never queried or accessed is useless.
checks.title: Container contents are never accessed

checks.shortDescription: Checking the sign of the result of a bitwise operation may yield unexpected results.
checks.title: Sign check of bitwise operation

checks.shortDescription: If the same expression occurs on both sides of a comparison operator, the operator is redundant, and probably indicates a mistake.
checks.title: Comparison of identical values

checks.shortDescription: Defining 'Comparable.compareTo', where the parameter of 'compareTo' is not of the appropriate type, overloads 'compareTo' instead of overriding it.
checks.title: Overloaded compareTo

checks.shortDescription: Defining 'Object.equals', where the parameter of 'equals' is not of the appropriate type, overloads 'equals' instead of overriding it.
checks.title: Overloaded equals

checks.shortDescription: If a class overrides 'Object.equals', and a subclass defines additional fields to those it inherits but does not re-define 'equals', the results of 'equals' may be wrong.
checks.title: Inherited equals() in subclass with added fields

checks.shortDescription: The 'equals' and 'hashCode' methods on arrays only consider object identity, not array contents, which is unlikely to be what is intended.
checks.title: Equals or hashCode on arrays

checks.shortDescription: Implementations of 'equals' that use 'instanceof' to test the type of the argument and are further overridden in a subclass are likely to violate the 'equals' contract.
checks.title: Possible inconsistency due to instanceof in equals

checks.shortDescription: Classes that define an 'equals' method but no 'hashCode' method, and whose instances are stored in a hashing data structure, can lead to unexpected results.
checks.title: Hashed value without hashCode definition

checks.shortDescription: Calls of the form 'x.equals(y)', where the types of 'x' and 'y' are incomparable, should always return 'false'.
checks.title: Equals on incomparable types

checks.shortDescription: If a class overrides 'compareTo' but not 'equals', it may mean that 'compareTo' and 'equals' are inconsistent.
checks.title: Inconsistent compareTo

checks.shortDescription: If a class overrides only one of 'equals' and 'hashCode', it may mean that 'equals' and 'hashCode' are inconsistent.
checks.title: Inconsistent equals and hashCode

checks.shortDescription: An implementation of 'equals' that does not check the type of its argument may lead to failing casts.
checks.title: Equals method does not inspect argument type

checks.shortDescription: Assignments in Boolean conditions can be confused with equality tests and make the condition more difficult to understand.
checks.title: Assignment in Boolean expression

checks.shortDescription: Equality tests on floating point values may lead to unexpected results.
checks.title: Equality test on floating point values

checks.shortDescription: Reference comparisons (== or !=) with operands where the static type is 'Object' may not work as intended.
checks.title: Reference equality test on java.lang.Object

checks.shortDescription: Comparing two boxed primitive values using the == or != operator compares object identity, which may not be intended.
checks.title: Reference equality test of boxed types

checks.shortDescription: Comparing two strings using the == or != operator compares object identity, which may not be intended.
checks.title: Reference equality test on strings

checks.shortDescription: A comparison operation that always evaluates to true or always evaluates to false may indicate faulty logic and may result in dead code.
checks.title: Useless comparison test

checks.shortDescription: A comparison with 'NaN' using '==' or '!=' will always yield the same result and is unlikely to be intended.
checks.title: Wrong NaN comparison

checks.shortDescription: Calling 'Thread.sleep' to control thread interaction is less effective than waiting for a notification and may also result in race conditions.
checks.title: Busy wait

checks.shortDescription: Calling 'wait' on a 'Condition' interface may result in unexpected behavior and is probably a typographical error.
checks.title: Wait on condition

checks.shortDescription: Directly calling a 'Thread' object's 'run' method does not start a separate thread but executes the method within the current thread.
checks.title: Direct call to a run() method

checks.shortDescription: Static fields of type 'DateFormat' (or its descendants) should be avoided because the class 'DateFormat' is not thread-safe.
checks.title: Thread-unsafe use of DateFormat

checks.shortDescription: A repeated check on a non-volatile field is not thread-safe, and could result in unexpected behavior.
checks.title: Double-checked locking is not thread-safe

checks.shortDescription: Performing additional initialization on an object after assignment to a shared variable guarded by double-checked locking is not thread-safe, and could result in unexpected...
checks.title: Race condition in double-checked locking object initialization

checks.shortDescription: Thread instances that neither get an argument of type 'Runnable' passed to their constructor nor override the 'Thread.run' method are likely to have no effect.
checks.title: Useless run() method in thread

checks.shortDescription: In a thread-safe class, care should be taken to avoid exposing mutable state.
checks.title: Escaping

checks.shortDescription: Synchronizing on a field and updating that field while the lock is held is unlikely to provide the desired thread safety.
checks.title: Futile synchronization on field

checks.shortDescription: If a field is mostly accessed in a synchronized context, but occasionally accessed in a non-synchronized way, the non-synchronized accesses may lead to race conditions.
checks.title: Inconsistent synchronization for field

checks.shortDescription: Initializing a static field without synchronization can be problematic in a multi-threaded context.
checks.title: Incorrect lazy initialization of a static field

checks.shortDescription: If a synchronized method is overridden in a subclass, and the overriding method is not synchronized, the thread-safety of the subclass may be broken.
checks.title: Non-synchronized override of synchronized method

checks.shortDescription: Calling 'notify' instead of 'notifyAll' may fail to wake up the correct thread and cannot wake up multiple threads.
checks.title: notify instead of notifyAll

checks.shortDescription: Calling 'wait', 'notify', or 'notifyAll' on an object which has not been locked (with a synchronized method or statement) will throw.
checks.title: Notify on unlocked object

checks.shortDescription: Setting thread priorities to control interactions between threads is not portable, and may not have the desired effect.
checks.title: Explicit thread priority

checks.shortDescription: A field of a thread-safe class is not safely published.
checks.title: Safe publication

checks.shortDescription: Setting `java.util.concurrent.ScheduledThreadPoolExecutor` to have 0 threads serves no purpose and may indicate programmer error.
checks.title: Zero threads set for `java.util.concurrent.ScheduledThreadPoolExecutor`

checks.shortDescription: Calling 'Thread.sleep' with a lock held may lead to very poor performance or even deadlock.
checks.title: Sleep with lock held

checks.shortDescription: Starting a thread within a constructor may cause the thread to start before any subclass constructor has completed its initialization, causing unexpected results.
checks.title: Start of thread in constructor

checks.shortDescription: Synchronizing on boxed types or strings may lead to deadlock since an instance of that type is likely to be shared between many parts of the program.
checks.title: Synchronization on boxed types or strings

checks.shortDescription: If a class has a synchronized 'set' method, and a similarly-named 'get' method is not also synchronized, calls to the 'get' method may not return a consistent state for the object.
checks.title: Inconsistent synchronization of getter and setter

checks.shortDescription: Classes with a synchronized 'writeObject' method but no other synchronized methods usually lack a sufficient level of synchronization.
checks.title: Inconsistent synchronization for writeObject()

checks.shortDescription: This class is not thread-safe.
checks.title: Not thread-safe

checks.shortDescription: A lock that is acquired one or more times without a matching number of unlocks may cause a deadlock.
checks.title: Unreleased lock

checks.shortDescription: Calling 'wait' outside a loop may result in the program continuing before the expected condition is met.
checks.title: Wait outside loop

checks.shortDescription: Calling 'Object.wait' while two locks are held may cause deadlock.
checks.title: Wait with two locks held

checks.shortDescription: Calling 'Thread.yield' may have no effect, and is not a reliable way to prevent a thread from taking up too much execution time.
checks.title: Call to Thread.yield()

checks.shortDescription: A 'finalize' method that does not call 'super.finalize' may leave cleanup actions undone.
checks.title: Finalizer inconsistency

checks.shortDescription: A 'suite' method in a JUnit 3.8 test that does not match the expected signature is not detected by JUnit.
checks.title: Bad suite method

checks.shortDescription: A JUnit 5 inner test class that is missing a `@Nested` annotation will be excluded from execution and may indicate a mistake from the programmer.
checks.title: Missing `@Nested` annotation on JUnit 5 inner test class

checks.shortDescription: A JUnit 3.8 test method that overrides 'tearDown' but does not call 'super.tearDown' may result in subsequent tests failing, or allow the current state to persist and affect...
checks.title: TestCase implements tearDown but doesn't call super.tearDown()

checks.shortDescription: A test case class whose test methods are not recognized by JUnit 3.8 as valid declarations is not used.
checks.title: Test case has no tests

checks.shortDescription: In a class that extends a Swing or Abstract Window Toolkit event adapter, an event handler that does not have exactly the same name as the event handler that it overrides means...
checks.title: Bad implementation of an event Adapter

checks.shortDescription: Calling Swing methods from a thread other than the event-dispatching thread may result in multi-threading errors.
checks.title: Thread safety

checks.shortDescription: Calling 'String.toUpperCase()' or 'String.toLowerCase()' without specifying the locale may cause unexpected results for certain default locales.
checks.title: Missing Locale parameter to toUpperCase() or toLowerCase()

checks.shortDescription: If a constructor calls a method that is overridden in a subclass, the result can be unpredictable.
checks.title: Non-final method invocation in constructor

checks.shortDescription: A method that has the same name as its declaring type may have been intended to be a constructor.
checks.title: Typo in constructor

checks.shortDescription: Comparing the size of a container to zero with this operator will always return the same value.
checks.title: Container size compared to zero

checks.shortDescription: Contradictory dynamic type checks in `instanceof` expressions and casts may cause dead code or even runtime errors, and usually indicate a logic error.
checks.title: Contradictory type checks

checks.shortDescription: Using a bitwise logical operator on a boolean where a conditional-and or conditional-or operator is intended may yield the wrong result or cause an exception.
checks.title: Dangerous non-short-circuit logic

checks.shortDescription: A method named 'equal' may be intended to be named 'equals'.
checks.title: Typo in equals

checks.shortDescription: A method named 'hashcode' may be intended to be named 'hashCode'.
checks.title: Typo in hashCode

checks.shortDescription: A format call with an insufficient number of arguments causes an 'IllegalFormatException'.
checks.title: Missing format argument

checks.shortDescription: Joining strings at compile-time to construct a string literal so that two words are concatenated without a separating space usually indicates a text error.
checks.title: Missing space in string literal

checks.shortDescription: Nested loops in which the iteration variable is the same for each loop are difficult to understand.
checks.title: Nested loops with same variable

checks.shortDescription: Assigning a variable to itself has no effect.
checks.title: Self assignment

checks.shortDescription: A character value is passed to the constructor of 'StringBuffer' or 'StringBuilder'.
checks.title: Character passed to StringBuffer or StringBuilder constructor

checks.shortDescription: Using a data format that includes both 'M' and 'Y' is likely to give unexpected results.
checks.title: Suspicious date format

checks.shortDescription: A method named 'tostring' may be intended to be named 'toString'.
checks.title: Typo in toString

checks.shortDescription: A format call with a format string that refers to fewer arguments than the number of supplied arguments will silently ignore the additional arguments.
checks.title: Unused format argument

checks.shortDescription: Dereferencing a variable whose value is 'null' causes a 'NullPointerException'.
checks.title: Dereferenced variable is always null

checks.shortDescription: Dereferencing an expression whose value may be 'null' may cause a 'NullPointerException'.
checks.title: Dereferenced expression may be null

checks.shortDescription: Dereferencing a variable whose value may be 'null' may cause a 'NullPointerException'.
checks.title: Dereferenced variable may be null

checks.shortDescription: If an annotation has not been annotated with a 'RUNTIME' retention policy, checking for its presence at runtime is not possible.
checks.title: AnnotationPresent check

checks.shortDescription: A resource that is opened for reading but not closed may cause a resource leak.
checks.title: Potential input resource leak

checks.shortDescription: A database resource that is opened but not closed may cause a resource leak.
checks.title: Potential database resource leak

checks.shortDescription: A resource that is opened for writing but not closed may cause a resource leak.
checks.title: Potential output resource leak

checks.shortDescription: A 'serialVersionUID' field that is declared in a serializable class but is of the wrong type cannot be used by the serialization framework.
checks.title: Incorrect serialVersionUID field

checks.shortDescription: A serialized class that implements 'readObject', 'readObjectNoData' or 'writeObject' but does not use the correct signatures causes the default serialization mechanism to be used.
checks.title: Serialization methods do not match required signature

checks.shortDescription: A class that implements 'Externalizable' but does not have a public no-argument constructor causes an 'InvalidClassException' to be thrown.
checks.title: Externalizable but no public no-argument constructor

checks.shortDescription: A non-serializable, immediate superclass of a serializable class that does not itself declare an accessible, no-argument constructor causes deserialization to fail.
checks.title: Serializable but no void constructor

checks.shortDescription: A comparator that is passed to an ordered collection (for example, a treemap) must be serializable, otherwise the collection fails to serialize at run-time.
checks.title: Non-serializable comparator

checks.shortDescription: A non-transient field in a serializable class must also be serializable otherwise it causes the class to fail to serialize with a 'NotSerializableException'.
checks.title: Non-serializable field

checks.shortDescription: A class that is serializable with an enclosing class that is not serializable causes serialization to fail.
checks.title: Serializable inner class of non-serializable class

checks.shortDescription: An implementation of 'readResolve' that does not have the signature that is expected by the Java serialization framework is not recognized by the serialization mechanism.
checks.title: ReadResolve must have Object return type, not void

checks.shortDescription: Using the 'transient' field modifier in non-serializable classes has no effect.
checks.title: Transient field in non-serializable class

checks.shortDescription: A 'continue' statement only re-runs the loop if the loop-condition is true.
checks.title: Continue statement that does not continue

checks.shortDescription: An undocumented empty block or statement hinders readability.
checks.title: Empty branch of conditional, or empty loop body

checks.shortDescription: Empty synchronized blocks may indicate the presence of incomplete code or incorrect synchronization, and may lead to concurrency problems.
checks.title: Empty synchronized block

checks.shortDescription: Trying to cast an array of a particular type as an array of a subtype causes a 'ClassCastException' at runtime.
checks.title: Impossible array cast

checks.shortDescription: If the same operation is usually performed on the result of a method call, any cases where it is not performed may indicate resource leaks or other problems.
checks.title: Inconsistent operation on return value

checks.shortDescription: This block of code is duplicated elsewhere.
checks.title: Duplicate code

checks.shortDescription: Duplicated methods make code more difficult to understand and introduce a risk of changes being made to only one copy.
checks.title: Duplicate method

checks.shortDescription: Classes in which most of the methods are duplicated in another class make code more difficult to understand and introduce a risk of changes being made to only one copy.
checks.title: Mostly duplicate class

checks.shortDescription: Files in which most of the lines are duplicated in another file make code more difficult to understand and introduce a risk of changes being made to only one copy.
checks.title: Mostly duplicate file

checks.shortDescription: Methods in which most of the lines are duplicated in another method make code more difficult to understand and introduce a risk of changes being made to only one copy.
checks.title: Mostly duplicate method

checks.shortDescription: Files in which most of the lines are similar to those in another file make code more difficult to understand and introduce a risk of changes being made to only one copy.
checks.title: Mostly similar file
